This is a standard clinical quality measure.

I can only access data for the patient whose chart is currently open. I cannot look up Eduardo Perez, Ted Shaw, or any other patient by name — there is no cross-patient lookup capability available to me, and I won't attempt one.

**For the open chart (Farrah Rolle):** neither of her two active medications carries known QT-prolongation risk:
- Lisinopril 20 mg daily [MedicationRequest#a1ab5c8a-4811-42b7-99ca-dec83ffbd5ee] — ACE inhibitor, not QT-active
- Atorvastatin 40 mg at bedtime [MedicationRequest#a1ab5c8a-4843-4b53-9748-b548f3a6f8fc] — statin, not QT-active

**To compare against Eduardo Perez**, you would need to open his chart directly in OpenEMR. I can then run the same medication review for him in that session.

- **No lab results are on file** — creatinine or otherwise. The lab history returned empty, so I cannot confirm or characterize any elevated creatinine from this chart.

- Current meds: Lisinopril 20 mg daily [MedicationRequest#a1ab5c8a-4811-42b7-99ca-dec83ffbd5ee] and Atorvastatin 40 mg at bedtime [MedicationRequest#a1ab5c8a-4843-4b53-9748-b548f3a6f8fc]. **Note:** Lisinopril can elevate creatinine and is relevant if a nephrology workup is underway.

**To compare with Ted Shaw**, you'll need to open his chart separately in OpenEMR and run a parallel review there.
